Record.statement_en: What will be the nature of charge on an atom when it loses an electron or when it gains an electrons?

Record.statement_en: State three reasons why do you think air is a mixture and water a compound ?
Record.statement_ur: آپ یہ کیوں کہتے ہیں کہ ہوا مکسچر ہے اور پانی کمپاؤنڈ؟ کم از کم تین وجوہات بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en: Air is called a mixture for the following reasons. 1) Air contains several gases in a specific ratio, including nitrogen, oxygen, argon and carbon dioxide. 2) These gases have their own properties. 3) These gases can be separated by common physical methods. 4) Air is oxygen. Cannot be expressed by chemical formula. Water is called a compound. There are following reasons for this. 1) Water consists of molecules and all molecules consist of two atoms of hydrogen and one atom of oxygen. It is a compound. 2) Water is a liquid and its properties are different from those of oxygen and hydrogen. 3) Hydrogen and oxygen in water cannot be separated by normal physical methods. 4) The chemical formula of water is H2O.

Record.statement_en: How does homogeneous mixture differ from heterogeneous mixture?
Record.statement_ur: ہومو جینیس مکسچر اور ہیٹرو جینیس مکسچر کیسے ایک دوسرے سے مختلف ہیں؟
Record.answer_en: All the components of a homogenous mixture are mixed equally and all the qualities of the whole mixture like color, smell, taste, hardness, physical state etc. are the same. Its particles are not felt separately. For example, water and salt mixture, syrup, cold drink etc. while the components of heterogeneous mixture are not mixed uniformly. And the physical properties of such mixtures are not uniform. Its components can be felt separately. For example, sand and gravel mixture, sand and salt mixture, water and soil mixture, etc.

Record.statement_en: Explain why are hydrogen and oxygen considered elements whereas water as a compound ?
Record.statement_ur: ہائڈروجن اور آکسیجن کو ایلیمیٹس اور پانی کو کمپاؤنڈ کیوں کہا جاتا ہے؟
Record.answer_en: Hydrogen and oxygen molecules consist of the same atoms, H2 and O2. There are such molecules while the water molecule is a heteroatomic molecule. It consists of oxygen and hydrogen atoms. Such molecules are molecules of compounds. So oxygen and hydrogen are elements and water is a compound.

Through this we cannot measure atoms. Therefore, the relationship between the number of atoms and the mass can be known through the mole. Record.statement_en: Differentiate between molecular mass and formula mass, which one of the following have molecular formula? H<sub>2</sub>O, NaCl, KI, H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>
Record.statement_ur: مالیکولر ماس  اور فارمولا ماس میں فرق واضح کریں۔ درج ذیل میں سے کون کون سے مالیکیولر فارمولا ہیں؟  H<sub>2</sub>O, NaCl, KI, H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>
Record.answer_en: Molecular Mass: The molecular mass is obtained by adding the atomic masses of all the atoms present in a molecule. The basic units of covalent compounds are molecules, so for covalent compounds we find the molecular mass. Formula Mass: The basic unit of an ionic compound is called formula unit. This is also the empirical formula of this compound. Adding the masses of the atoms in a formula unit gives the formula mass. Find the formula mass for ionic compounds. The molecular formulas of H2SO4 and H2O are mass. Find their molecular masses. While Kl and NaCl are ionic compounds, they have empirical formulas and will determine their formula masses.

Record.statement_en: What is the significance of symbol of an element?
Record.statement_ur: ایلیمنٹ کو سمبل سے لکھنے کا کیا فائدہ ہے؟
Record.answer_en: By writing the elements with the help of symbols, the chemical reactions between them can be easily written. In this way the formulas of compounds can also be written easily. Because of writing the full name, different types of molecules can be easily identified, while it will be difficult to know the difference between different compounds of two elements. For example, hydrocarbons consist of carbon and hydrogen only. In case of full name Lakhnai, it will be difficult to understand their difference.

Record.answer_en: Industrial Chemistry: The branch of chemistry which deals with the methods of making chemical compounds on a commercial scale is called industrial chemistry. Record.answer_en: Biochemistry studies the structure, synthesis and chemical factors of chemical substances found in living organisms. Biochemistry is the study of metabolism in living organisms. Record.answer_en: Atomic Mass Unit: 1/12th of the total mass of one atom of carbon-12 is called atomic unit. This was necessary because atoms are very small in size and their mass could not be determined directly. Therefore, an equally small scale was needed to determine their masses. This requirement was fulfilled in the form of atomic mass unit.

Record.statement_en: How is periodicity of properties dependent upon number of protons in atom ?
Record.statement_ur: خصوصیات کی پیریاڈیسٹی کس ایٹم میں موجود پروٹونز کی تعداد پر منحصر ہوتی ہے؟
Record.answer_en: The nuclear charge changes due to the number of protons. As nuclear charge increases in a period, size decreases and properties change. Similarly, the size of an atom increases with increasing nuclear charge in a group. The features also change with it.

Record.statement_en: Why shielding effect of electrons makes cation formation easy?
Record.statement_ur: الیکٹرون کا شیلڈنگ ایفکیٹ ، کیٹائن کے بننے کے عمل کو کیوں آسان بنا دیتا ہے؟
Record.answer_en: As the shielding effect of electrons increases, the effective nuclear charge decreases. In this way, the force of attraction between the nucleus and the valence electrons is reduced, thus the process of forming cation becomes easier.

Record.statement_en: Why and how elements are arranged in 4<sup>th</sup> period ?
Record.statement_ur: ایلیمنٹس کو چوتھے پیریڈ میں کیوں اور کیسے ترتیب دیا گیا ہے؟
Record.answer_en: There are total eighteen elements in the fourth period. The first two elements are in the s block. After these ten elements (Sc or Zn) are transition elements. After these are again six elements of p block. All of them are placed in the fourth period because their electrons are distributed in four shells.

Record.statement_en: Why the size of atom does not decrease regularly in a period?
Record.statement_ur: ایک پیریڈ میں ایٹم کا سائز باقاعدگی سے کم کیوں نہیں ہوتا؟
Record.answer_en: The reason the size of an atom decreases irregularly in a period is due to the changing electronic configuration. Atomic size tends to vary when electrons are in fully or half-filled subshells. If there are unequally filled subshells, the atomic size trend is different.

Record.statement_en: Why noble gases are not reactive ?
Record.statement_ur: نوبل گیسز کیوں ری ایکٹو نہیں ہوتیں؟
Record.answer_en: Noble gases have two or eight electrons in their valence shell. Thus, due to the completion of their valence shell, they do not want to absorb or emit any more electrons. For this reason, they have a very low tendency of chemical reactivity.

Record.statement_en: Why does a covalent bond become a polar?
Record.statement_ur: ایک کوویلنٹ بانڈ پولر کیوں بن جاتاہے ؟
Record.answer_en: When a covalent bond is formed between two different atoms having different electronegativity values, the covalent bond is called a polar covalent bond. The electron pair forming in the covalent bond will be inclined towards the atom whose electronegativity is higher. As a result, the atom with higher electronegativity has a partial positive charge and the atom with less electronegativity has a partial positive charge, and then the molecule becomes a polar molecule and the bond is polar covalent. A bond is formed.

Record.statement_en: Why does ice float on water?
Record.statement_ur: برف پانی پر کیوں تیرتی ہے؟
Record.answer_en: If the water is cooled, the water takes the form of ice due to hydrogen bonding. In this process, the average distance of water molecules increases by moving to certain places. As a result, the density of ice is less than that of water, which is why ice floats on the surface of water.

Record.statement_en: Give the characteristic properties of the ionic compound.
Record.statement_ur: آئیونک کمپانڈز کی مخصوص خصوصیات بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en: The properties of an ionic compound are as follows. (1) Although these compounds are made of ions, they are generally reactive. (2) Such compounds are often soluble in water and insoluble in non-aqueous solutions. (3) In aqueous solutions, these compounds are found in the form of free ions. Therefore, electric current can easily pass through them. Similarly, even in the molten state, these ions are isolated. (4) These compounds have very high melting and boiling points. (5) Ionic compounds are very stable (6) They are highly charged.

Record.statement_en: What characteristic properties do the covalent compound have?
Record.statement_ur: کوویلنٹ کمپاؤنڈ میں کون سی مخصوص خصوصیات پائی جاتی ہیں؟
Record.answer_en: 1) Covalent compounds have lower melting and boiling points than ionic compounds. (2) Covalent compounds of low molecular mass exist as gas or liquid. (3) Such compounds have a more polar solubility, i.e. they do not dissolve in water but dissolve in non-polar solutions. (4) They are generally electrically non-conductive. (5) They are neither hard nor full.

Record.statement_en: What do you mean by evaporation? how it is affected by surface area?
Record.statement_ur: ایویپوریشن سے کیا مراد ہے؟ سطحی رقبہ کا اس پر کیا اثر ہوتا ہے؟
Record.answer_en: The process of converting a liquid into vapors is called evaporation. This process takes place only at the surface of liquids. Therefore, when the surface area of â€‹â€‹the liquid is more, the evaporation of the liquid will be more and if the surface area of â€‹â€‹the liquid is less, the evaporation will be less. Example: By pouring equal amount of water or alcohol into a plate, beaker and narrow mouthed bottle. put it. The plate has the largest surface area of â€‹â€‹the liquid, so the most liquid will evaporate from there. The lowest liquid will then evaporate from the beaker and into the bottle.

Record.statement_en: Define the term allotropy with examples.
Record.statement_ur: ایلو ٹروپی کی مثالیں دے کر بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en: The occurrence of an element in different forms in the same physical state is called allotropy. Allotropy has the following causes. (1) The presence of two or more forms of an element with different numbers of atoms, such as the allotropes of oxygen, oxygen and ozone. (2) Crystals of an element exhibit allotropy due to the different arrangement of more than two dozen atoms or molecules. For example, the different arrangement of molecules in sulfur crystals exhibits allotropy.

Record.statement_en: How is electroplating of tin on steel carried out?
Record.statement_ur: سٹیل پرٹِن کی الیکٹروپلیٹنگ کیسے کی جاتی ہے؟
Record.answer_en: Generally, steel cotton is kept in a tank containing tin electrolyte for plating. The steel is connected to an electrical circuit that acts as the cathode while the tin electrode acts as the anode. When a current passes through the circuit, the tin ions in the solution become radii and freeze on the steel.

Record.answer_en: Passing a current through an electrolytic cell causes a chemical reaction that results in the formation of cations and anions. Catines accumulate at the cathode and anions at the anode. Due to release of extra electrons from anions, the anode becomes negatively charged. A chemical reaction in a galvanic cell produces a current. In a galvanic cell, electrons move from the zinc electrode to the copper electrode. As a result, a positive charge is placed on the anode.

Record.statement_en: Why is steel plated with nickel before the electroplating of chromium?

Record.answer_en: Chromium does not bond well directly to the steel surface. Moreover, moisture can pass through it, causing the metal to peel off. Therefore, nickel to copper plating is done first on steel surface for strength and convenience as nickel or copper has greater adhesion strength on steel surface. This is followed by chromium plating. This type of electroplating prevents rust better.

Record.statement_en: Differentiate between oxidizing and reducing agents.
Record.statement_ur: آکسیڈائزنگ اور ریڈیو سنگ ایجنٹس کے درمیان فرق بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en: Oxidizing Agent: An oxidizing agent is a species that oxidizes an object by taking electrons from it. Thus, the object (atom or ion) that emits electrons itself is called an oxidizing agent. For example, non-metals are oxidizing agents because they gain electrons because they are more electronegative elements. Radiusing Agent: A radiating agent is a species that radiates an object by donating electrons. Thus, the substance (atom or ion) which oxidizes itself by releasing electrons is also called reducing agent. Almost all metals are good reducing agents because they tend to give up electrons.

Record.statement_en: What is the difference between valency and oxidation state?
Record.statement_ur: ویلنسی اور آکسیڈیشن سٹیٹ میں کیا فرق ہے؟
Record.answer_en: The number of electrons an atom or ion absorbs or emits during a chemical reaction is called its valency. When writing valency, number is written first and then charge. Record.statement_en: How electropositivity depends upon size and nuclear charged of an atom?
Record.statement_ur: الیکٹرو پوزٹیویٹی کا انحصار ایٹم کے سائز اور نیوکلیئر چارج پر کیسے ہے؟
Record.answer_en: Going from left to right in a period, the nuclear charge increases while the number of shells remains the same. Therefore, the size of the atom decreases continuously from left to right. Due to smaller size, the valence shells are closer to the nucleus and it becomes difficult to eject electrons from them, thus increasing the nuclear charge decreases the electropositivity. Electropositivity increases as atomic size increases from top to bottom in a group because the effective nuclear charge decreases and the number of shells increases down the group.

Record.statement_en: Why the second ionization energy of magnesium is higher than the first one?

Record.answer_en: After the removal of the first electron from the magnesium atom, the number of electrons in metals becomes 11. While the number of protons in the nucleus is only 12, thus the nuclear charge increases for each electron and as a result the force of attraction of the nucleus increases, so it becomes difficult to eject the second electron, so the second ionization energy is greater than the first ionization energy. .

Record.statement_en: What is the reason for the difference between solutions, colloids and suspensions?
Record.statement_ur: سلوشنز ،کولائڈز اور سسپنشنز میں فرق کی کیا وجہ ہے؟
Record.answer_en: The components of solutions are so small that light passing through them does not scatter. Whereas the components of colloids are slightly larger. When light passes through them, they scatter the light. But its components are not large enough to be seen by the naked eye. The particles in suspension are so large that they block light, so it is very difficult for light to pass through them.

Record.answer_en: NaCl is an ionic compound while water is a polar compound. When NaCl is dissolved in water, its ions are separated and water molecules being polar easily dissolve them in themselves thus forming a solution.

Record.statement_en: What do you mean, like dissolve like? Explain with examples.
Record.statement_ur: Like dissolves like  کا کیا مطلب ہے مثالوں سے وضاحت کریں۔
Record.answer_en: Like dissolves like means that solute and solvent of similar nature will form a solution. That is, polar and ionic compounds will dissolve in polar and ionic solvents. For example, the solubility of sodium chloride and sugar in water. Whereas non-polar compounds will dissolve in non-polar solvents. For example, the solubility of naphthalene in gasoline, the solubility of grease in Mobil oil.

Record.statement_en: Why suspensions and solutions do not show tyndall effect, while colloids do?
Record.statement_ur: سسپنشنز اور سلوشنز ٹنڈل ایفکیٹ کا مظاہرہ کیوں نہیں کرتے ؟جب کہ کولائڈز کرتے ہیں۔
Record.answer_en: The components of the solution are very small and do not scatter light. Whereas the components of suspensions are so large that they can be seen with the naked eye and light cannot pass through them because they are undissolved in the liquid. While the particles of colloids are larger than the particles of the solution and smaller than the particles of the suspension and they scatter the light, this is called the Tyndall effect.
